zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de la Lee於1364年出生在英國賀特福爾阿爾布瑞時，她的父親Sir John de Lee26歲，母親Joan Pateshull22歲。她和John Barley至少有1個兒子。她去世於1420年在英國英格蘭德比夏爾享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de la Lee于1364年出生在英国賀特福爾阿爾布瑞时，她的父亲Sir John de Lee26岁，母亲Joan Pateshull22岁。她和John Barley至少有1个儿子。她去世于1420年在英国英格兰德比夏爾享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de la Lee was born in 1364, in Albury, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Sir John de Lee, was 26 and her mother, Joan Pateshull, was 22. She had at least 1 son with John Barley. She died in 1420, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de la Lee est née en 1364, à Albury, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Lee, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joan Pateshull, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John Barley. Elle est décédée en 1420, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de la Lee 1364 in Albury, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Lee 26 und ihre Mutter Joan Pateshull 22. Sie hatte mit John Barley mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1420 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Lee è nata il nel 1364 nel Albury, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Lee, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Joan Pateshull, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John Barley. Morì nel 1420, nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de la Leeが1364年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアアルブリーで生まれた時，父Sir John de Leeは26歳，母Joan Pateshullは22歳であった。彼女はJohn Barleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1420年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de la Lee(이)가 1364년 영국 허트퍼드실르 알버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Lee(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Pateshull(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 John Barley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Lee nasceu em 1364, em Albury, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Lee, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Joan Pateshull, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John Barley. Ela faleceu em 1420, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de la Lee родилась в 1364 г. в местности албури, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Lee было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Pateshull, 22. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John Barley. Она умерла в 1420 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de la Lee nació en 1364, en Albury, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Lee, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Joan Pateshull, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con John Barley. Murió en 1420, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 56 años.
